methods.description: Procedure for sampling groundwater and analyzing noble gas concentrations, isotope ratios, and tritium content using mass spectrometry and the 3He ingrowth method, including age calculation and comparison with input functions.

steps.description: In 2006, a subset of the monitoring wells was sampled along a vertical transect for noble gas and tritium ( 3 H) analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: sample groundwater monitoring wells along a vertical transect

steps.description: After purging the wells, the samples were taken using a submersible pump.
To avoid degassing of the groundwater due to bubble formation during sampling the water was pumped at high rates to maintain high pressure.
steps.searchable_summary: pump groundwater samples at high rates using a submersible pump

steps.description: The samples for noble gas and 3 H analysis were put into copper tubes and sealed gastight using pinch-off clamps.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer groundwater samples into copper tubes and seal gastight

steps.description: All samples were analyzed for noble gas concentrations and the isotope ratios 3 He/ 4 He, 20 Ne/ 22 Ne, and 36 Ar/ 40 Ar using noble gas mass spectrometry in the Noble Gas Laboratory at ETH Zurich (30,33).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze noble gas concentrations and isotope ratios using mass spectrometry

steps.description: 3 H concentrations were determined by the 3 He ingrowth method using a high-sensitivity compressor-source noble gas mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine tritium concentrations using the 3He ingrowth method and mass spectrometry

steps.description: 3 H-3 He ages were calculated according to the equations listed in (34), taking into account an excess air correction.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate tritium-helium ages from tritium and helium concentrations

steps.description: When comparing the reconstructed original 3 H content of each sample as a function of 3 H-3 He age with the 3 H input function for South and Southeast Asia (Fig. S5), most samples follow the trend expected from simple plug flow (34)(35).
steps.searchable_summary: compare reconstructed tritium content vs age with tritium input function

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing groundwater samples for trace elements like Arsenic, Iron, and Manganese using high-resolution inductively-coupled plasma mass spectrometry (HR ICP-MS).

steps.description: Several days before analysis by high-resolution inductively-coupled plasma mass spectrometry (HR ICP-MS) at LDEO, groundwater was acidified to 1% Optima HCl in the laboratory (36). This has been shown to entirely re-dissolve any precipitates that could have formed (37).
steps.searchable_summary: acidify groundwater samples with HCl before HR ICP-MS analysis

steps.description: In most cases, the difference between duplicates was within the analytical van uncertainty of ~5%.
steps.searchable_summary: perform duplicate analysis and check analytical uncertainty

steps.description: With the exception of needle-sample data and the nest of 10 wells in the Holocene portion of the aquifer, which had to yield to construction, groundwater As, Fe, and Mn concentrations reported here represent the average for samples collected without filtration in April and May 2012.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze acidified groundwater samples for As, Fe, and Mn using HR ICP-MS and average concentrations

methods.method_name: Dissolved Organic and Inorganic Carbon Analysis (DOC/DIC)
methods.description: Procedure for collecting and analyzing groundwater samples for Dissolved Organic Carbon (DOC) and Dissolved Inorganic Carbon (DIC) using a TOC analyzer.

steps.description: Dissolved organic carbon (DOC) samples were collected in 25 mL glass vials combusted overnight at 450 °C and acidified to 1% HCl at the time of collection.
steps.searchable_summary: collect DOC samples in pre-combusted vials and acidify with HCl

steps.description: Dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) samples were also collected in 25 mL glass vials with a Teflon septum but were not acidified.
steps.searchable_summary: collect DIC samples in glass vials with septum

steps.description: Both DOC ("NPOC") and DIC (by difference of "TC-NPOC") were analyzed on a Shimadzu TOC-V carbon analyzer calibrated with K phthalate standards.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze DOC (NPOC) and DIC (TC-NPOC) using a TOC analyzer

methods.description: Procedure for collecting, preserving, and analyzing groundwater samples for Ammonium concentration using spectrophotometry.

steps.description: Ammonium samples were collected in polypropylene bottles after passing through 0.45 um cellulose acetate membrane filters and preserved by acidifying to pH<2 with HNO 3 .
steps.searchable_summary: collect ammonium samples, filter, and acidify with HNO3

steps.description: NH 4 + concentrations were analyzed on a spectrophotmeter (UV-3101, Shimadzu) at a wavelength of 690 nm after forming complex with nitroferricyanide (38).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ammonium concentrations using spectrophotometry after complex formation

methods.description: Procedure for collecting, preserving, and analyzing groundwater samples for Methane concentration using gas chromatography.

steps.description: Methane (CH 4 ) samples were filled up to about half of the pre-vacuumed glass vials and immediately frozen in dry ice.
steps.searchable_summary: collect methane samples in pre-vacuumed vials and freeze immediately

steps.description: The analyses were performed no longer than 10 days after sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze methane samples within 10 days of collection

steps.description: Headspace CH 4 in the vials was measured on a Shimadzu 2014 gas chromatography with a Porapak T packed column (14).
steps.searchable_summary: measure headspace methane concentration using gas chromatography
